summary: Policy-backed control for whether Windows Search indexes encrypted files. Useful for people who need fast search inside encrypted content, but it carries security caveats and rebuilds the index when changed.

setting.name: Indexing of Encrypted Files
setting.casual_explanation: Windows Search can be told to index encrypted files so their contents are searchable. That can make searching easier, but it also means Windows needs to process decrypted content during indexing.
setting.why_people_change_it: People enable it when they want full-text search over encrypted files. They leave it off when they want the safer default or do not need encrypted-content indexing.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Enabling it makes encrypted content more searchable, but Microsoft warns that the index location should be protected with full-volume encryption and that the index is rebuilt when the setting changes.
setting.professional_notes: Search.admx maps AllowIndexingEncryptedStoresOrItems under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Search with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0. Search.adml says enabling it attempts to decrypt and index encrypted content, disabling it tells search components not to index encrypted items, and not configuring it uses the local Control Panel setting, which defaults to not indexing encrypted content.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows uses the local search setting, which by default does not index encrypted content.

allowed_values.label: Do not index encrypted files
allowed_values.meaning: Search components are expected not to index encrypted items or encrypted stores.

allowed_values.label: Index encrypted files
allowed_values.meaning: Indexing attempts to decrypt and index encrypted content while still respecting access restrictions.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes AllowIndexingEncryptedStoresOrItems = 1, which matches the documented enabled state.

windows_defaults.label: Windows policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows systems exposing the Windows Search policy
windows_defaults.notes: If not configured, Windows uses the local Search setting. The local ADML help text says that default local behavior is to not index encrypted content.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Systems that do not need full-text search over encrypted files
avoid_for: Users who need encrypted-file contents indexed

states.rationale: Leave the policy unset and keep the safer local default.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You do not need encrypted file contents to be indexed.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You need Windows Search to find content inside encrypted files.
tradeoffs: Encrypted content is not indexed by default.
Search over encrypted files remains limited.

recommended_profiles.label: Search encrypted content
intended_for: Workstations that rely on encrypted-file search
avoid_for: Systems without full-volume protection for the index location
General-purpose systems that do not need this feature

states.rationale: Allow Windows Search to index encrypted items.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You need searchable contents inside encrypted files.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You prefer the safer default and do not need encrypted-content indexing.
tradeoffs: Changing the setting rebuilds the index.
Microsoft recommends full-volume encryption for the index location.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft Search.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps AllowIndexingEncryptedStoresOrItems under the Windows Search policy key with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft Search.adml help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text explains that enabling the policy indexes encrypted content, disabling it blocks encrypted indexing, not configuring it uses the local setting, and the default local behavior is to not index encrypted content.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes AllowIndexingEncryptedStoresOrItems = 1 for the current tweak.

decision.why: Microsoft policy files document the registry mapping, values, default behavior, and security caveats clearly, and the app writes the documented enabled value with a clean restore path.

validation_proof.notes: Local Microsoft Search.admx defines the exact machine policy path and value name. The companion Search.adml help text explains that enabling the policy indexes encrypted content, disabling it blocks encrypted indexing, and not configuring it falls back to the local setting.
